entities.name: GENDER
entities.description: Gender-differentiated roles and benefits in technology adoption and resource allocation

entities.name: SOCIAL CAPITAL
entities.description: The buildup of social resources and networks that mediate technology uptake

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY ADOPTION
entities.description: The process of taking up and implementing new agricultural technologies

entities.name: DECISION-MAKING
entities.description: Patterns of men and women regarding production, consumption, and resource allocation

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop produced in the study areas of Maharashtra

entities.name: MAHARASHTRA
entities.description: The state in western India where the case study was conducted

entities.name: WESTERN INDIA
entities.description: The region in India containing Maharashtra

entities.name: GROUNDNUT PRODUCTION TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The package of production technology introduced in the region in 1987

entities.name: SOCIAL NETWORKS
entities.description: The networks of relationships that influence technology adoption

entities.name: GENDER-BASED ACTIVITY PATTERN
entities.description: The patterns of activities performed by men and women in agriculture

entities.name: FARMER WELFARE
entities.description: The overall well-being of farmers and the farming community

entities.name: FORMAL NETWORKS
entities.description: Networks reflecting employment or occupation status, predominantly used by men

entities.name: INFORMAL NETWORKS
entities.description: Networks centered on family and kin, predominantly used by women

entities.name: BONDING SOCIAL CAPITAL
entities.description: Strong bonds among family members or ethnic groups, typically developed by women

entities.name: BRIDGING SOCIAL CAPITAL
entities.description: Weaker, crosscutting ties with acquaintances and different groups, typically developed by men

entities.name: EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITIES
entities.description: Job prospects for women that improved with the introduction of technology

entities.name: WOMEN EMPOWERMENT
entities.description: The process of increasing the autonomy and power of women through technology and social capital
